title: nassella lachnophylla
intro: Nassella lachnophylla, also known as Needle-and-thread grass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, as well as promoting wound healing. Nassella lachnophylla can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its dried roots and leaves can be used in herbal preparations, including tinctures and poultices.
